no_filter.label: A: No filter (full scan)
no_filter.description: Full 10M-row scan + 500K hash build — optimizer sees no filter
no_filter.plan_excerpt:
EXPLAIN ANALYZE          SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS cnt, SUM(o.revenue) AS total         FROM orders o         JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id         GROUP BY p.category         ORDER BY total DESC     
│        Aggregates:        │
│         HASH_JOIN         │
│      Join Type:

single_predicate.label: B: Region filter (~20% selectivity)
single_predicate.description: WHERE region='East' prunes ~80% rows before hash join
single_predicate.plan_excerpt:
EXPLAIN ANALYZE          SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS cnt, SUM(o.revenue) AS total         FROM orders o         JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id         WHERE o.region = 'East'         GROUP BY p.category         ORDER BY total DESC     
│        Aggregates:        │
│         HASH_

double_predicate.label: C: Double predicate (~2% selectivity)
double_predicate.description: WHERE region='East' AND revenue>90 prunes ~98% rows
double_predicate.plan_excerpt:
EXPLAIN ANALYZE          SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS cnt, SUM(o.revenue) AS total         FROM orders o         JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id         WHERE o.region = 'East'           AND o.revenue > 90.0         GROUP BY p.category         ORDER BY total DESC     
│        Aggre

comparison.optimizer_insight: 1 predicate → 1.27x speedup (region prunes ~80% rows); 2 predicates → 1.57x speedup (prunes ~98% rows); DuckDB optimizer pushes predicates below the join operator

validation.lecture: CMU 15-721 Lectures 07-08: Query Optimization, Cost-Based Optimization
validation.concept: Predicate pushdown + HASH_JOIN plan selection based on table statistics
validation.proof: No filter: 120ms (baseline); 1 predicate: 95ms (1.27x speedup, ~20% selectivity); 2 predicates: 77ms (1.57x speedup, ~2% selectivity); Join operator: ['HASH_JOIN'] in all scenarios
validation.validates: L07-08 claim: optimizer pushes predicates below join operators to prune rows before the hash build phase; selectivity × row_count = estimated output cardinality used for plan costing
validation.interpretation: Adding a region filter (20% selectivity) prunes 80% of the 10M-row fact table before the hash join, yielding 1.27x speedup. A second predicate (revenue > 90) drops selectivity to ~2%, giving 1.57x total speedup. The optimizer detects ['HASH_JOIN'] as the correct plan for an equi-join — it never falls back to nested-loop. L07-08 insight: 'push predicates as deep as possible before build/probe phases.'

maps_to: CMU 15-721 Lectures 07-08: Query Optimization, Cost-Based Optimization
